commit.message:
Add tool calling support via suspending SDK handlers

Tool calling bridge:
- ToolCallBridge converts Anthropic tool defs → SDK custom tools
- Handlers suspend (return unresolved Promise) when model invokes a tool
- tool_use content blocks streamed back in Anthropic SSE format
- Next request with tool_result resolves the handler, SDK resumes

SessionManager updates:
- Tracks tool bridges and pending tool call state per session
- Sessions with pending tool calls stay alive (not cleaned up)
- Tool result continuation routing via hasPendingToolCalls flag

Messages handler:
- Detects tool_result blocks in incoming messages
- Routes to pending session for continuation
- Supports text + tool_use mixed responses
- Streaming and non-streaming tool flows

Tests: 62 passing (11 new for tool-call-bridge)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.description: 🛠️ A Python-based tool for installing, configuring, and managing GitHub Copilot plugins. 🤖

commit.message:
Add comprehensive unit tests (51 tests)

Test suites:
- message-formatter: extractTextContent, extractSystemMessage, formatMessagesAsPrompt (16 tests)
- sse: SSE headers, all event types, streaming lifecycle, response skeleton (13 tests)
- session-manager: creation, reuse, divergence detection, concurrency, TTL, shutdown (19 tests)
- server: health check, model listing, request validation (3 tests)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(buffer): replace time.After polling with sync.Cond in AcquireForWriting

Replace inefficient 5ms polling loop in ring buffer acquisition with a
sync.Cond broadcast mechanism, eliminating unnecessary wake-ups and latency
under compositor back-pressure.

- Remove time.After-based polling in AcquireForWriting
- Add sync.Cond to Ring struct, signaled by MarkReleased
- Rename tryAcquireSlot to tryAcquireSlotLocked for clarity
- Update context cancellation handling with context.AfterFunc
- Add sync.Once to GPUBackend for one-time nil-atlas warnings
- Refactor internal/wayland/input tests for improved coverage
- Update README.md to correct demo binary count (22→18)
- Remove AUDIT.md (results incorporated into fixes)

This eliminates up to 5ms of latency per frame and reduces CPU overhead
during compositor back-pressure scenarios.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Update README with auth docs, session reuse details, and corrected config

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(#1): :sparkles: Utilizar apenas iniciais do nome no Avatar

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Eldad Hauzman <ehauzman@gmail.com>

Implement session reuse with delta tracking

Replace session-per-request with SessionManager that:
- Fingerprints conversations by model + system + first message
- Reuses sessions when new requests extend existing conversations
- Only sends the delta (new user messages) to reused sessions
- Detects history divergence and creates fresh sessions
- Auto-cleans idle sessions after 5 minute TTL
- Handles concurrency (busy sessions get parallel instances)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- internal/render/atlas: add 16 new tests for image region allocation
  * TestAllocateImageRegion_ExistingPage

  * TestAllocateImageRegion_MaxPagesNoEviction
  * TestEvictOldestRegion_SingleRegion
  * TestEvictOldestRegion_MultipleRegions
  * TestCompactAtlas_SinglePage
  * TestCompactAtlas_Fragmentation
  * And 8 more covering edge cases and error conditions
  Increases coverage from 27.0% to ~70%

- internal/wayland/input: add 24 new tests for input event handling
  * TestKeyboard_HandleEvent_Enter/Leave/Key/Modifiers
  * TestPointer_HandleEvent_Enter/Leave/Motion/Button
  * TestTouch_HandleEvent_Down/Up/Motion
  * Helper functions for building wire.Argument test fixtures
  Increases coverage from 25.0% to ~60%

- AUDIT.md: mark atlas coverage remediation as complete [x]

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: implement error classification and smart recovery in headless runner (#259)

* Initial plan

* feat: implement error classification and smart recovery in headless runner

- Add classify_error(output, exit_code, http_status) function to error_classifier.rs
- Add transient_backoff_secs field to RalphConfig (default 30s)
- Wire classify_error into headless.rs validation failure paths
  - Transient: sleep configurable backoff (default 30s) before retry
  - NeedsReplan: immediately reset task to pending and go to NeedsTrigger
- Fix pre-existing missing validation_command/error_classifier in main.rs
- Update all RalphConfig struct literal instances with new field

Co-authored-by: randymarsh77 <1606704+randymarsh77@users.noreply.github.com>

- Move anyhow::{Context, Result} import under #[cfg(test)] in semantic_eval.rs

Co-authored-by: randymarsh77 <1606704+randymarsh77@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix merge ralph: prevent duplicate conflict issues and unblock draft PR advancement (#582)

* Initial plan

* Fix merge ralph: guard against duplicate work and fix draft PR advancement

Two bugs fixed:

1. Added guard in run_merge to check for existing pending issues or tracked
   PRs before creating new conflict resolution work. This prevents duplicate
   issues from being filed for the same merge conflict.

2. Updated the Draft PR handler in advance_tracked_prs to also check
   is_pr_work_completed() when the agent is still assigned to the issue.
   Some agents (e.g. Copilot) remain assigned after finishing, so the
   two-tier check (session completion + title heuristic) is now consulted
   before deciding to skip. This mirrors the same pattern already used in
   check_agent_status.

Also made is_pr_work_completed public so it can be called from headless.rs.

Co-authored-by: randymarsh77 <1606704+randymarsh77@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Complete PLAN.md Steps 4-7: coverage, shader CI gate, a11y tests, debt tracking

Step 4: Raise wain root package coverage from ~54% to 60.0%
- Add TestImageToBufferOversizedImage (primitives.NewBuffer error path)
- Crosses the ≥60% acceptance criterion from 59.9%

Step 5: GPU shader-to-ISA compilation CI gate
- render-sys/Cargo.toml: add 'rlib' to crate-type to enable integration tests
- render-sys/tests/shader_compile.rs: 6 integration tests covering all 7 WGSL
  shaders compiled through naga → Intel EU (Gen9/11/12) and AMD RDNA (1/2/3)
  lowering passes; assert non-empty, 16-byte-aligned binaries
- .github/workflows/ci.yml: add 'Run shader-to-ISA compilation CI gate' step
- HARDWARE.md: add code-verified vs hardware-validated distinction and updated
  CI testing matrix with Shader→ISA rows

Step 6: internal/a11y tests with -tags=atspi
- internal/a11y/manager_test.go: 30+ headless unit tests covering all four
  AT-SPI2 interface wrappers, object setters, and D-Bus-free Manager paths
- Coverage: 38.1% → 74.2% (target: ≥70%)

Step 7: Update TECHNICAL_DEBT.md
- TD-3 (GPU shader CI gate): In Progress → Resolved
- TD-4 (AT-SPI2 build tag docs): Open → Resolved
- TD-6 (a11y zero test coverage): Open → Resolved

Delete PLAN.md: all 7 steps complete.

* Initial plan

Co-authored-by: sbsmith86 <1700409+sbsmith86@users.noreply.github.com>

* Hardcode Anthropic base URL in scorer to prevent Epic gateway leak

The shell-level ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL (Epic Portkey gateway) was overriding
dotenv, routing Scout scoring requests to the wrong endpoint. Hardcoding
the base URL in the Anthropic client constructor ensures Scout always
hits the Anthropic API directly regardless of shell environment.

Also adds assets/ directory with Scout icon files.

Fix NBA API headers to bypass bot detection in all NBA data gens

- Added required NBA-specific headers: x-nba-stats-origin, x-nba-stats-token, Origin, Sec-Fetch-*

- Retry backoff increased: 5s/10s/15s instead of flat 2s
- kaggle_nba_gen.py and nba_stream_sync.py: added missing headers (had none)

commit.message:
fix: treat mergeable_state=blocked as NotMergeable to detect failing checks (#618)

* Initial plan

* fix: treat mergeable_state=blocked as NotMergeable to detect failing checks

When GitHub reports mergeable=true but mergeable_state=blocked, the PR
has failing required status checks. Previously check_pr_merge_status
returned Mergeable in this case, skipping the failing-checks detection
and @copilot comment. Now it returns NotMergeable so the existing
logic properly checks for failures and requests a fix.

Co-authored-by: randymarsh77 <1606704+randymarsh77@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: randymarsh77 <1606704+randymarsh77@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add Notion-to-pipeline key mapping in read.js

Read functions now return snake_case keys (id, source, org, status)
matching the sheets module interface, so the pipeline and dashboard
can use the Notion backend as a drop-in replacement without any
call-site changes.

maps (OPPORTUNITIES_MAP, LEADS_MAP, CORRECTIONS_MAP) that translate

commit.message:
Update sample-api submodule: add UI stress test coverage

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor(next): split next.ts into next/ subdirectory

Split the 3,384-line src/client/next.ts into four focused files
under src/client/next/:

- helpers.ts: shared internal utilities (isRecord, hasFunctionRefCandidate,
  readMember, readFunctionRef, hasPluginFunctionRefs, ConvexRouteFunctionRef)
- plugins.ts: plugin types and factories (NextAuthApiPlugin,
  NextAuthApiPluginFactory, coreApiPlugin, pluginApiPlugin, etc.)
- client.ts: client types and factories (NextAuthClientOptions,
  createNextAuthClient, createNextReactAuthClient, etc.)
- index.ts: server-side code (request helpers, cookie/OAuth helpers,
  server factories) + re-exports from plugins.ts and client.ts

Update package.json exports from ./src/client/next.ts to
./src/client/next/index.ts (and dist equivalents).

feat: Add complete 4art CLI infrastructure with poster generation

- Add bin/poster.js: Main CLI entry point with 6 commands (generate, list, search, info, batch, status)
- Add src/core/4art-generator.js: Main poster generation orchestrator
- Add src/core/4art-json-generator.js: Data orchestration from geo-index.json
- Add src/core/canvas-renderer.js: Canvas-based PNG rendering with Mercator projection
- Add src/core/batch-generator.js: Concurrent batch processing
- Add src/core/4art-list.js, 4art-search.js, 4art-info.js: Command handlers
- Add src/core/status.js: System diagnostics
- Add src/core/headless-renderer.js: Headless rendering support
- Update package.json: Add CLI dependencies (yargs, axios, canvas, csv-parse)
- All 12 tests passing, 520 artists supported, Mercator projection for markers

This is the /4art fork - a standalone CLI tool for generating artist location posters.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
